patches.title: CairoSVG vulnerable to Exponential DoS via recursive <use> element amplification
patches.description:
## Summary

Kozea/CairoSVG has exponential denial of service via recursive `<use>` element amplification in `cairosvg/defs.py` (line ~335). This causes CPU exhaustion from a small input.

patches.title: Netmaker has Insufficient Authorization in Host Token Verification
patches.description: The Authorize middleware in Netmaker incorrectly validates host JWT tokens. When a route permits host authentication (hostAllowed=true), a valid host token bypasses all subsequent authorization checks

patches.title: OpenClaw's Signal reaction-only status events could, in limited cases, be enqueued before access checks
patches.description:
### Summary
In a narrow Signal reaction-notification path, reaction-only inbound events could enqueue a status event before sender access checks were applied.

patches.title: mageMagick has a possible use-after-free write in its PDB decoder
patches.description:
A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the PDB decoder that will use a stale pointer when a memory allocation fails and that could result in a crash or a single zero byte write.
